title: AI data center developers target rural territory to bypass city construction bans and regulations rural locations allow sites to bypass city council approvals, rezoning votes, land-use reviews, and reduce public scrutiny
summary: Data center projects are shifting to rural areas to bypass municipal regulations, reducing permits and public scrutiny. These sites offer larger parcels, allowing for more space to build various structures. This shift affects fewer people and reduces regulatory friction.
insight: Rural data centers bypass city regulations and reduce public scrutiny.

title: Intel, SK hynix shares surge following reports of chip packaging partnership SK is said to be testing Intel's 2.5D EMIB for HBM integration
summary: SK hynix shares surge after reports of a potential chip packaging partnership with Intel, focusing on the use of Intel's 2.5D EMIB technology for HBM integration.
insight: Advancements in 2.5D packaging could boost memory technology efficiency.

title: Save $210 on this 9800X3D bundle that includes 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM and a 1TB Samsung 9100 Pro SSD start your AM5 build for just $979 and get a free power supply
summary: Get a powerful 9800X3D PC bundle with RAM, SSD, and PSU for $979.
insight: High-performance PC bundle at an affordable price point.

title: IRGC-linked media outlines plan to tax and control undersea internet cables in the Hormuz Strait Iran's mouthpiece calls for a cut of $10 trillion of transactions that pulse through the cables daily
summary: The Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil and shipping route, may become a key location for controlling internet cables, with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps-linked media calling for a tax on transactions passing through the region.
insight: Iran seeks to exert control over global digital commerce via Hormuz.

title: Portable 40mm launcher kills drones by firing 6.5-feet-long steel chains at 80 m/s German researchers' low-tech mechanical 'bola' outshines textile drops quadcopters without lasers or EMPs
summary: German researchers developed a low-tech mechanical launcher that fires steel chains to take down drones, utilizing the same physics as a traditional bola. This method is effective in disabling quadcopters without the use of lasers or electromagnetic pulses. The launcher's simplicity and effectiveness make it a notable innovation.
insight: Simple physics outperforms complex technology in drone countermeasures

title: MIT researchers revive 40-year-old triangular zipper concept now made possible by 3D printing, creates shape-shifting robots and deployable structures 3D-printed 'Y-Zipper' turns floppy tentacles into rigid beams in seconds
summary: MIT researchers developed a 3D-printed 'Y-Zipper' that transforms floppy structures into rigid beams.
insight: Revolutionizing deployable structures and robots with rapid transformation capabilities.

title: Former Epic director is building a European rival to the Unreal and Unity game engines 'The Immense Engine' dev sees opportunity for AI agents to 'do the work of ten or fifteen people'
summary: A European games engine, 'The Immense Engine', is in development, aiming to rival Unreal and Unity. The project is led by a veteran from Epic Games. It focuses on utilizing AI for enhanced efficiency.
insight: AI can automate tasks, reducing development costs and time

title: China's Hanyuan-2 debuts as 'world's first' dual-core quantum computer 200-qubit claims incredible power efficiency, but lacks critical performance benchmarks
summary: China's Hanyuan-2 is a dual-core quantum computer with 200 qubits, boasting high power efficiency.
insight: Breakthrough in quantum computing paves way for potential exponential computing power.

title: 3DMakerPro Toucan 3D Scanner review: All-in-one 3D scanning
summary: The 3DMakerPro Toucan is a standalone 3D scanner that captures high-quality point clouds and 3D models. It boasts a premium feel and fast scanning capabilities, but its software has room for improvement. However, the device is still a powerful tool for 3D scanning and modeling.
insight: Standalone 3D scanning for on-site model creation and export

title: AI data center project secretly sucked 29 million gallons of water over 15 months before detected by residents complaining about low water pressure officials refuse to fine builders of massive 6.2 million-square-foot facility over unauthorized water use
summary: A massive 6.2 million-square-foot AI data center secretly used 29 million gallons of water over 15 months. The water was consumed for temporary construction activities such as concrete work and site preparation. The builders of the facility were not fined for unauthorized water use.
insight: Excessive water usage in data centers poses significant environmental concerns

title: Tiny credit card computer includes eInk screen and is just 1mm thick Muxcard is powered by the ESP32-C3 microcontroller
summary: The Muxcard is a credit card-sized computer with an eInk screen, powered by the ESP32-C3 microcontroller.
insight: Miniaturization of computing technology enables unprecedented portability and versatility.

title: AMD's legendary K5, its first independently-designed processor, is being removed from the Linux kernel 4.3-million-transistor chip gets the axe because it lacks Time Stamp Counter (TSC) support, making it a coding burden
summary: AMD's K5 processor family will be removed from Linux kernel 7.2 due to a lack of Time Stamp Counter (TSC) support, which poses a significant coding burden. This move is part of the kernel's continuous effort to modernize and simplify its hardware support. As a result, users with K5-based systems may experience compatibility issues.
insight: TSC support is a critical requirement for modern kernel compatibility.

title: AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 vs Ryzen 9 9950X3D faceoff How far does dual cache take you?
summary: The AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2's dual cache is put to the test against its single-cache counterpart, the Ryzen 9 9950X3D, in a series of six challenging tasks. The results show the impact of having an extra cache on performance. We'll explore how far the extra cache takes the Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 in this face-off.
insight: Dual cache delivers significant performance boosts in cache-intensive applications.

title: Louis Rossmann tells 3D printer maker Bambu Lab to Go (Bleep) yourself over its threatened lawsuit against enthusiast Right to Repair advocate offers to pay the legal fees for a threatened OrcaSlicer developer
summary: Louis Rossmann offers $10,000 to help a developer threatened by Bambu Lab's cease and desist letter. He calls on the Right to Repair community to crowd-fund the developer's legal defense. This move is seen as a stand against restrictive practices.
insight: Right to Repair advocates push against restrictive manufacturer control.

title: AI data centers face increasing complaints about inaudible but 'felt' infrasound citizens complain high- and low-frequency sounds do not register on decibel meters but cause adverse health effects
summary: Residents near data centers complain about low-frequency noise pollution from 24/7 operations, which can cause adverse health effects despite not registering on decibel meters.
insight: Infrasound health impacts go beyond traditional decibel noise measurement ranges.

title: $200 'socketed' Nvidia AI GPU for servers hacked into a PCIe card with custom PCB and 3D-printed cooling modded Tesla V100 SMX data center GPU runs AI LLMs and is more efficient than many modern midrange offerings in AI inference
summary: Nvidia's V100 SMX GPU, costing $200, outperforms midrange AI offerings efficiently.
insight: Old GPU tech can still deliver high performance at lower costs.

title: NASA pushes Mars helicopter rotors past the speed of sound for the first time ever next-gen SkyFall aircraft's rotors hit 3,750 RPM, ten times faster than normal helicopters
summary: NASA successfully tested Mars helicopter rotors at Mach 1.08, breaking sound barrier, for the first time. This achievement paves the way for larger next-generation aircraft under the proposed SkyFall mission. The SkyFall mission aims to explore more of the Red Planet.
insight: Advancing Mars aviation technology for more extensive planetary exploration.

title: RVA23-compliant K3 Pico-ITX SBC and K3-CoM260 SoM feature SpacemiT K3 octa-core RISC-V AI SoC, up to 32GB RAM, 256GB UFS
summary: SpacemiT has launched the K3 Pico-ITX SBC and K3-CoM260 SoM with the SpacemiT K3 octa-core RISC-V AI SoC, supporting up to 32GB RAM and 256GB UFS.
insight: RISC-V AI SoC offers high performance and flexibility in edge AI applications.

title: Mind xPlay display and keyboard review using Khadas Mind and Mind 2 mini PCs
summary: This review evaluates the Khadas Mind xPlay display and keyboard using mini PCs, including comparisons to a standard laptop screen. The Mind xPlay provides noticeable improvements in display quality over other screens. Various tests and comparisons demonstrate its capabilities.
insight: Khadas Mind xPlay delivers improved display quality over standard laptop screens

title: Up to 256 MB FERRIT modular F-RAM storage device preserves critical data for up to 200 years
summary: Machdyne's FERRIT is a modular F-RAM storage device with up to 256 MB capacity, capable of preserving data for up to 200 years with virtually unlimited writes and high-radiation resistance.
insight: Revolutionary storage solution for critical data with unparalleled durability and reliability.

title: Pi Slate A Raspberry Pi 5 handheld Linux cyberdeck with a 5-inch 1920 720 touchscreen display
summary: The Pi Slate is a compact, modular handheld cyberdeck designed for security-focused applications, featuring a Raspberry Pi 5 processor, 5-inch touchscreen display, and up to 16GB of RAM.
insight: Enhanced cybersecurity capabilities for professionals in a portable package.

title: Chip revenue to reach record $1.3T in 2026, including sensors
summary: Gartner forecasts chip revenue to reach a record $1.3T in 2026, driven by sensors and other applications. This growth is expected despite memory price inflation, which will primarily impact non-AI demand. The AI sector will continue to drive growth.
insight: Memory price inflation threatens non-AI chip demand severely

title: A data center used 29 million gallons of water without a bill, while residents complained about low water pressure
summary: A county investigation revealed a data center's unauthorized water usage, causing low pressure for residents.
insight: Unmonitored water connections can lead to significant unauthorized consumption.

title: How Apple Pay Works: The Tech Behind
summary: Apple Pay uses a complex chain of cryptography and tokenization for secure contactless payments.
insight: Secure payments rely on real-time authorization and sophisticated cryptography solutions.

title: Former Epic director and Guerrilla Games co-founder is building a European game engine to rival Unreal and Unity
summary: Arjan Brussee, a former Epic director and Guerrilla Games co-founder, is developing The Immense Engine, a European game engine to rival Unreal and Unity.
insight: European game engine rival seeks to break US-dominated gaming tech

title: A cyberattack on Canvas knocked out access for students at Harvard, Columbia, and hundreds of other schools during finals
summary: A cyberattack on Canvas platform affected Harvard, Columbia, and hundreds of schools during finals, exposing sensitive student data.
insight: Personal student data compromised due to platform cybersecurity breach.
